title: Beam canalization by a non-Abelian gauge field
authors: Olha Bahrova, Jiahao Ren, Feng Jin, Rui Su, Guillaume Malpuech, Dmitry Solnyshkov
abstract: Hyperbolic and quasi-flat isofrequency contours (IFCs) are used for beam canalization and can be created by tilted Dirac points in photonic systems. Dirac points in microcavities are generated by the combination of transverse-electric/transverse-magnetic splitting and linear birefringence. We show that the canalization is here strongly assisted by the coupling between the spatial dynamics and polarization pseudospin precession. This dynamics is well described analytically and numerically as the action of a non-Abelian gauge field on emergent charges (spin current). We demonstrate a ten-fold enhancement of the canalization for a Gaussian beam by the gauge field, as compared to a description based solely on the group velocity associated with the IFCs.
summary: This paper shows that a non-Abelian gauge field can significantly enhance the canalization of light beams in microcavities. By coupling spatial motion to polarization precession, the gauge field provides a Lorentz-like force that suppresses beam spreading. This effect leads to a ten-fold improvement in beam collimation compared to standard predictions.
main_problem: Investigating how a non-Abelian gauge field, arising from the coupling between spatial dynamics and polarization pseudospin precession, affects the canalization (beam collimation) of light in photonic systems.
main_result: The study demonstrates a ten-fold enhancement of beam canalization for a Gaussian beam due to the non-Abelian gauge field compared to descriptions based solely on group velocity. It also identifies an anti-symmetric pattern of circular polarization accompanying this effect.
method: The authors use a combination of semi-classical trajectories of wavepacket components and direct numerical solutions of the spinor Schrodinger equation in real space, utilizing a Hamiltonian formalism to derive equations of motion.
model_or_system: The system consists of planar microcavities where TE-TM splitting and linear birefringence create tilted Dirac points. The dynamics are modeled using a Yang-Mills theory for SU(2) where the system acts as a matter field coupled to an emergent non-Abelian gauge potential.
key_observables: Total polariton intensity (beam profile), Stokes vector component s3 (circular polarization degree), pseudospin precession, and Zitterbewegung-like oscillations.
important_parameters: TE-TM splitting strength (beta), optical birefringence (beta0), in-plane wavevector (k), and initial Gaussian beam width.
main_assumptions: The analytical approach assumes the s3 spin component evolution is equivalent to a nonlinear Duffing oscillator and treats the x-direction wavevector as approximately constant (qx approx 1).
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows different isofrequency contour types, beam canalization simulations, and the anti-symmetric s3 pattern. Fig 2 displays the evolution of spin projection, momentum, and transverse coordinates, highlighting the beam shift with and without the gauge field.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the concept of beam canalization via isofrequency contours, presents a mathematical model using non-Abelian gauge fields and Hamiltonian dynamics, provides numerical simulations of beam propagation and spin evolution, and concludes with a comparison of the gauge-field-enhanced effect versus standard group velocity models.
why_interesting: The paper explores the emergence of non-Abelian gauge fields and Yang-Mills dynamics in a photonic platform, which is highly relevant to researchers interested in synthetic gauge fields, topological photonics, and the manipulation of pseudospin-orbit coupling.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Schematic representation of main types IFCs marked by thick curves: hyperbolic (blue), flat (red) and parabolic (green). The dashed curves shows the correspond- ing inner band IFCs. The gray curves plotted within the high- lighted sector indicate additional IFCs. (b) Total polariton intensity, obtained from the SE simulation, and normalized along the x axis manifesting pronounced canalization effect strongly enhanced by the present effective non-Abelian gauge field. (c) s3 Stokes vector component.

FIG. 2. a) The spin projection s3(t) for a single period: nu- merical and analytical solutions. b) The qy(t) projection: nu- merical and analytical solutions. c) The transverse coordinate as a function of time y(t) (numerics, y∗is the oscillation am- plitude). d) Final shift with and without the gauge field as a function of initial wave vector qy(0) (numerics).

FIG. 3. The effect of the gauge field. a) Transverse coordinate y as a function of time t for different starting points without gauge field (gray lines) and with gauge field (color – s3). b) The canalization effect of the gauge field g for different initial beam size ∆qy.

Spin-momentum entanglement.note: The paper's central result is the coupling between spatial dynamics (beam profile) and polarization pseudospin precession, which is a direct study of spin-momentum entanglement in a photonic system.
